items.correct: Three sections: A, contrasting B, and a return to A (ABA).
items.why: Students assume 'three sections' means three different ones. Ternary has only TWO distinct ideas, but A returns - it's the return that makes it ternary, not the section count alone.

items.q: What is the function of the bridge in a pop song?
items.correct: To provide contrast before the final chorus, often by changing melody, key, or texture.
items.misconception: It is the same as the chorus.
items.random_wrong: It is the very first section.
items.why: Students confuse bridge with chorus because both are 'big' sections. The bridge appears once (usually after the second chorus) to provide CONTRAST and renew interest before returning.

items.q: How does D Dorian differ from D minor?
items.correct: Dorian has a raised 6th (B natural) compared to natural minor.
items.random_wrong: Dorian has no sharps or flats at all.
items.why: Students treat modes as variants of major/minor. Dorian on D uses only white keys (like C major, but starting on D), giving a raised 6th compared to D natural minor - producing the modal flavour.

items.q: Which 1969 Miles Davis album is foundational jazz fusion?
items.why: It's Bitches Brew. “Kind of Blue” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which 1986 Paul Simon album featured South African musicians?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Graceland. “Hearts and Bones” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year did Peter Gabriel found WOMAD?
items.why: Exact answer: 1982 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
